title: YouTube Fix, Bug Report Button, In-App Messaging Tabs, User ID Messaging, Global Reply, and Admin Pause/Resume

requirements.text: Fix the YouTube posting integration: diagnose and resolve the infinite loading spinner in the YouTube post flow. Ensure that when a user clicks 'Post to YouTube' on a ClipCard, the request completes (success or error) and the spinner is dismissed. Verify that the clip is actually submitted to the connected YouTube channel via the backend HTTP outcall, and that success/failure toast notifications are shown.

quotes: Stop the infinite loading spinner and enable posting to channels.

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Post to YouTube' no longer shows an infinite spinner — the button resolves within a reasonable timeout.
A successful post shows a success toast; a failed post shows an error toast with a human-readable message.
The clip is submitted to the user's connected YouTube channel via the backend.
If no YouTube channel is connected, a clear error message is shown instead of a spinner.

requirements.text: Re-add the 'Report a Bug / Request a Feature' button to the main UI. The button should open the existing FeedbackModal component. It must be visible to all authenticated users and placed in a consistent, discoverable location (e.g., the SideNavigation sidebar and/or the AppShell bottom navigation on mobile).

quotes: Re-add the 'Report a Bug / Request a Feature' button to the UI.

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Report a Bug / Request a Feature' button is visible to all authenticated users in the sidebar (desktop) and bottom nav or floating button (mobile).
Clicking the button opens the FeedbackModal dialog.
The modal allows submission of a bug report or feature request as before.

requirements.text: Update the UserMessages component so that each message item opens in a new internal tab within the app (using the existing TanStack Router tab or an in-app tab mechanism), rather than navigating away or opening an external browser tab. Additionally, replace any external download links in the app with an in-app download mechanism: when a user triggers a file/clip download, use the useDownloadClip hook to fetch the URL and initiate a browser download directly (e.g., via a temporary anchor element with the `download` attribute), without redirecting to an external website.

quotes: Open all messages in a new internal tab and enable in-app file downloads.

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a message opens it in a new in-app tab or detail panel without leaving the application.
Downloading a clip does not redirect to an external website — the file download is triggered directly in the browser via a programmatic anchor click.
The download button in ClipCard uses the useDownloadClip hook result to trigger the download without navigation.

requirements.text: Update the messaging system so that all messages display and are sent using the user's generated User ID (the 8-character alphanumeric ID from userIdGenerator.ts) rather than raw principal strings. In the backend, store and index messages by principal but surface the short User ID in all frontend displays. Additionally, add a global 'Reply' feature to UserMessages so that any authenticated user can reply to a message they received — the reply should be sent back to the original sender using their User ID, and the reply should be stored and visible in the conversation.

quotes: Use 'User IDs' for all messages and enable a global 'Reply' feature.

acceptanceCriteria: All message displays (sent and received) show the short User ID (8-char alphanumeric) instead of full principal strings.
The send-message form identifies senders by their User ID label.
Every message in UserMessages has a 'Reply' button/input that allows the recipient to send a reply back to the original sender.
Replies are stored in the backend and visible in the message thread.
The backend AdminMessage type or a new Message type supports a reply/thread structure.

requirements.text: Add a Pause/Resume application switch to the Admin Panel (AdminPanel.tsx / SystemControls.tsx area). When the app is 'Paused', non-admin/non-owner users see a maintenance/paused screen and cannot use the app. Toggling this switch must require the user to be authenticated as an Admin or Owner — if an unauthenticated or non-admin user tries to access the toggle, they must be shown an admin login prompt. The current pause state must be persisted in the backend (stable variable). A 'Resume' action restores normal access. The existing SystemControls component should be updated or extended to include this toggle.

quotes: Add a Pause/Resume switch that requires an Admin login to toggle.

acceptanceCriteria: A Pause/Resume toggle switch is visible in the Admin Panel for admin/owner users.
Toggling requires the user to be authenticated as admin or owner; otherwise an admin login prompt is shown.
When the app is paused, regular (non-admin, non-owner) users see a 'App is currently paused' maintenance screen instead of the normal UI.
Admin and owner users can still access the admin panel while the app is paused.
The pause state is stored in a stable backend variable and persists across canister upgrades.
The backend exposes setPauseState(paused: Bool) and getAppStatus() methods, protected by admin authorization.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor.
Generate migration.mo only if stable state variables are added or changed (e.g., pause state).
User IDs displayed in the frontend must use the existing userIdGenerator.ts utility — do not introduce a new ID scheme.
nonGoals: No external email or SMS notifications for paused/resumed state.
No new third-party OAuth providers beyond the existing Google OAuth for YouTube.
No real-time WebSocket messaging — polling is acceptable.
No changes to the Internet Identity authentication flow.
No redesign of the overall visual theme or layout.
